steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three places where mosaics are found: libraries, train stations, and public squares. The blank falls between "libraries" and "train stations," so it must supply the correct punctuation to separate items in a list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'libraries: train': A colon introduces an explanation or definition, not a list item. Using a colon here breaks the list incorrectly. ✗
  • Choice B — 'libraries train': No punctuation between "libraries" and "train" makes the two words run together as if they form a single unit. ✗
  • Choice C — 'libraries—train': A dash signals an interruption or aside, not a standard separator between list items. ✗
  • Choice D — 'libraries, train': A comma correctly separates the first item from the second in a three-item list (libraries, train stations, and public squares). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
